channel.title: The Kiwi Dialectic — Course Events
channel.description: Course lesson events across The Kiwi Dialectic.

item.title: Course launch
item.description: Gramsci for Aotearoa — Course overview and how to follow along.

item.title: Lesson 1: Who was Gramsci, and why read him now?
item.description: Gramsci for Aotearoa — Introduction to Gramsci's life, ideas, and relevance today.

item.title: Lesson 2: Hegemony — how ruling ideas become ordinary life
item.description: Gramsci for Aotearoa — Why ruling ideas become ordinary life.

item.title: Lesson 3: Organic Intellectuals — who speaks for the working class?
item.description: Gramsci for Aotearoa — Who speaks for the working class and how?

item.title: Lesson 4: Civil Society, the State, and who controls the institutions
item.description: Gramsci for Aotearoa — How institutions reproduce power — and how to fight back.

item.title: Lesson 5: War of Position — building counter-power from below
item.description: Gramsci for Aotearoa — The long game of building socialist counter-power.

item.title: Lesson 6: Putting it all together — a socialist toolkit for Aotearoa
item.description: Gramsci for Aotearoa — Applying Gramsci's framework to Aotearoa New Zealand today.

item.title: Lesson 1: Who was Kropotkin, and what is mutual aid?
item.description: Kropotkin & Mutual Aid — An introduction to cooperation beyond capitalist myth-making.

item.title: Course intro — free email course from The Kiwi Dialect
item.description: Kropotkin & Mutual Aid — Overview and how to enrol.

item.title: Lesson 2: Mutual Aid vs Social Darwinism — nature is not a war of all against all
item.description: Kropotkin & Mutual Aid — The idea that humans are naturally selfish is ideology, not science.

item.title: Lesson 3: Mutual Aid in Working-Class History — strikes, friendly societies, and solidarity networks
item.description: Kropotkin & Mutual Aid — Working-class people have always built mutual aid networks to survive capitalism.

item.title: Lesson 4: Mutual Aid in Aotearoa — hapū, whānau, working-class communities
item.description: Kropotkin & Mutual Aid — Mutual aid is not a Western import to Aotearoa. It was already here.

item.title: Lesson 5: The State vs Mutual Aid — when welfare replaced self-organised solidarity
item.description: Kropotkin & Mutual Aid — The welfare state was a genuine gain — and also used to dismantle self-organisation.

item.title: Lesson 6: Mutual Aid Now — from food banks to tenants unions to climate response
item.description: Kropotkin & Mutual Aid — Mutual aid is not theoretical. It is happening right now.

item.title: Course intro — free email course from The Kiwi Dialect
item.description: Graeber: Debt, Bullshit Jobs & Direct Democracy — Six lessons on Graeber's big ideas — free. Enrol here.

item.title: Course intro — coming soon
item.description: Gilles Deleuze for Educators, Artists, and Organisers — Overview of the upcoming Deleuze course.

item.title: Course intro — He Ako mo ngā Umanga Mahi Ngātahi
item.description: He Ako mo ngā Umanga Mahi Ngātahi — How to Start a Cooperative in Aotearoa — How to start a cooperative in Aotearoa — overview and how to follow along.

item.title: Lesson 1: Who was Graeber, and why does anthropology make you dangerous?
item.description: Graeber: Debt, Bullshit Jobs & Direct Democracy — Anthropology, anarchism, and seeing the world differently.

item.title: Lesson 2: Debt — the first 5,000 years and why you should never feel morally obligated to a bank
item.description: Graeber: Debt, Bullshit Jobs & Direct Democracy — How debt was invented as a tool of domination long before capitalism.

item.title: Lesson 3: Bullshit Jobs — why so much work under capitalism is meaningless by design
item.description: Graeber: Debt, Bullshit Jobs & Direct Democracy — Why most work under capitalism is pointless by design.

item.title: Lesson 4: The Utopia of Rules — why bureaucracy is a problem for the left, not just the right
item.description: Graeber: Debt, Bullshit Jobs & Direct Democracy — How paperwork, means-testing, and administration humiliate and exhaust people.

item.title: Lesson 5: The Dawn of Everything — what the deep past tells us about human freedom
item.description: Graeber: Debt, Bullshit Jobs & Direct Democracy — What Occupy Wall Street and other movements actually built.

item.title: Lesson 6: Synthesis — what Graeber's work means for thinking and acting in Aotearoa today
item.description: Graeber: Debt, Bullshit Jobs & Direct Democracy — Debt, work, bureaucracy, and direct democracy through a New Zealand lens.

item.title: Module 1 — Whakapapa o te raraunga
item.description: Te Pā Tūwatawata — AI, tikanga & data sovereignty — The whakapapa of data — where does data come from, and who does it belong to?

item.title: Module 2 — Te Pā Tūwatawata hei tauira
item.description: Te Pā Tūwatawata — AI, tikanga & data sovereignty — Te Pā Tūwatawata as a model for Indigenous-led digital infrastructure.

item.title: Module 3 — AI me te raupatu matihiko
item.description: Te Pā Tūwatawata — AI, tikanga & data sovereignty — AI and digital extraction — tracing the colonial logic inside machine learning.

item.title: Module 4 — Tikanga, ture, mana whakahaere
item.description: Te Pā Tūwatawata — AI, tikanga & data sovereignty — Tikanga, law, and governance — how Māori legal frameworks challenge tech power.

item.title: Module 5 — Hoahoa tika
item.description: Te Pā Tūwatawata — AI, tikanga & data sovereignty — Designing ethical systems — what does a decolonised AI architecture look like?

item.title: Module 6 — He anamata rangatira
item.description: Te Pā Tūwatawata — AI, tikanga & data sovereignty — Sovereign digital futures — building Māori-controlled infrastructure for generations.

item.title: Course launch — AI Warrior
item.description: AI Warrior — The Kiwi Dialectic — AI Warrior goes live: a working-class course in AI literacy, six modules, six thinkers, one praxis. Train the mind. Arm the class.

item.title: Course launch — Tangi o te Tāheke
item.description: Tangi o te Tāheke — Bakunin mō Aotearoa — The cry of the waterfall. Why Bakunin, why Aotearoa, why now — and how this course closes the AI Warrior thinkers series.

item.title: Lesson 1: Ko wai a Bakunin? — the man Marx feared
item.description: Tangi o te Tāheke — Bakunin mō Aotearoa — The Russian who split the First International, defended the Paris Commune, and warned that any state — even a workers' state — becomes a new prison.

item.title: Course launch — AI Literacy for Families
item.description: AI Literacy for Families — a 6-week household course — Why a working-class AI literacy course for families. The household agreement, the workbook, the SIFT cheat sheet.

item.title: Lesson 2: Mana motuhake — federation from below, not the state from above
item.description: Tangi o te Tāheke — Bakunin mō Aotearoa — Bakunin's free federation read alongside He Whakaputanga and tino rangatiratanga. Power binds upward from the hapū, never downward from Wellington.

item.title: Week 1 — How AI actually works
item.description: AI Literacy for Families — a 6-week household course — Pattern-matching, not magic. Predict, classify, generate.

item.title: Lesson 3: Te tāheke — the passion for destruction is also a creative passion
item.description: Tangi o te Tāheke — Bakunin mō Aotearoa — How to tear down without nihilism. The waterfall as kaupapa: what falls feeds the awa, what is demolished irrigates the kāinga.

item.title: Week 2 — Bias & deepfakes
item.description: AI Literacy for Families — a 6-week household course — Structural problems with the same root.

item.title: Lesson 4: Caracoles in the kāinga — a Zapatista reading for Aotearoa
item.description: Tangi o te Tāheke — Bakunin mō Aotearoa — Chiapas to Ōtepoti. Autonomous councils, mandar obedeciendo, and what a Kiwi caracol looks like — marae, co-ops, learning circles, mutual-aid pātaka.

item.title: Week 3 — Privacy & ethics
item.description: AI Literacy for Families — a 6-week household course — Who owns the conversation when you talk to a chatbot.

item.title: Lesson 5: Refusing the algorithmic Leviathan — anarchism in the age of AI
item.description: Tangi o te Tāheke — Bakunin mō Aotearoa — Bakunin called the state a machine. The machine is now literal. How federation, encryption, and refusal apply to platform capital and state AI.

item.title: Week 4 — Critical thinking & fact-checking
item.description: AI Literacy for Families — a 6-week household course — The SIFT method.

item.title: Lesson 6: Arm the class — closing the AI Warrior thinkers series
item.description: Tangi o te Tāheke — Bakunin mō Aotearoa — From Gramsci to Bakunin in six thinkers. The toolkit, the refrain, the kaupapa for the next decade. E kore e mau te rongo ki te whenua kāore e watea.

item.title: Week 5 — Setting household digital boundaries
item.description: AI Literacy for Families — a 6-week household course — Co-written, not imposed. The Household AI Agreement.

item.title: Course launch — Pedagogy of the Algorithm
item.description: Pedagogy of the Algorithm — Freire mō Aotearoa — Why Freire, why now. Every chatbot is a banking-model classroom. Freire's antidote, mapped onto kura kaupapa Māori praxis. Six lessons across six weeks.

item.title: Week 6 — Modeling responsible tech habits
item.description: AI Literacy for Families — a 6-week household course — Parents commit too. Closing the loop.

item.title: Lesson 1: Ko wai a Freire? — the teacher who refused to deposit
item.description: Pedagogy of the Algorithm — Freire mō Aotearoa — Recife 1921 to São Paulo 1997. The man whose literacy method taught sugar-cane workers to read the word and the world — and got him 70 days in a military prison for it.

item.title: Lesson 2: The banking model goes industrial — every chatbot is a deposit
item.description: Pedagogy of the Algorithm — Freire mō Aotearoa — Freire's banking concept of education, retold for the age of generative AI. What happens when the teacher is the model and the deposit is your prompt history.

item.title: Lesson 3: Problem-posing — how to force the chatbot to think with you
item.description: Pedagogy of the Algorithm — Freire mō Aotearoa — From banking to dialogue. Problem-posing as a praxis — the prompts, the discipline, the refusal to be a passive depositor of someone else's training data.

item.title: Lesson 4: Conscientização — critical consciousness in the algorithmic feed
item.description: Pedagogy of the Algorithm — Freire mō Aotearoa — Freire's central concept. Reading the word and the world when the world is a recommendation algorithm. How conscientização becomes a daily discipline, not a one-off awakening.

item.title: Lesson 5: Kura kaupapa Māori — Aotearoa's own refusal of the banking model
item.description: Pedagogy of the Algorithm — Freire mō Aotearoa — Graham Hingangaroa Smith's kaupapa Māori praxis read alongside Freire. Te reo revitalisation as a Freirean act. The pedagogy that built itself when the Crown's would not teach it.

item.title: Lesson 6: A Freirean praxis for the AI moment — what we teach next
item.description: Pedagogy of the Algorithm — Freire mō Aotearoa — Closing the loop. A working-class, kaupapa-aligned, AI-aware Freirean praxis. Generative themes for organisers, study circles, and the household. What we carry into Deleuze.
